channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Greenland's leader says his people don't want to be Americans as Trump covets territory
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Greenland's prime minister said Friday that the mineral-rich Arctic territory's people don't want to be Americans, but that he understands U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US hits Venezuela with new sanctions after Maduro's 'illegitimate' inauguration
item.description: The Biden administration announced a slew of new sanctions against allies of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro in opposition to his claim of election victory, calling his Friday inauguration “illegitimate” and a “desperate attempt to seize power.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Salvage complete of tanker at center of oil spill scare after being hit by Houthi rebels in Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — An oil tanker that burned for weeks in the Red Sea after being attacked by Yemen's Houthi rebels and threatening a massive oil spill has been salvaged, a security firm said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Estonia steps up patrols in the icy Baltic Sea in a show of force after suspected cable sabotage
item.description: ABOARD THE EML SAKALA IN THE BALTIC SEA (AP) — As they plied the gray, icy waters of the Baltic Sea west of Russia on Thursday, the crew of the Estonian minehunter EML Sakala kept a careful eye on any vessels slowing down suspiciously or suddenly changing course.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS and HENDRIK OSULA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US finds no 'Havana syndrome' link to foreign powers, but 2 spy agencies say it's possible
item.description: A U.S. intelligence report finds no evidence linking a foreign power to the mysterious “Havana syndrome” injuries experienced by U.S. personnel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump setting up meeting with Putin, in communication with Xi
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said he is working on setting up a meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin for after his Jan. 20 inauguration and is in "communication" with Chinese President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Puerto Rico's chief for natural resources shelves investigation targeting governor's in-laws
item.description: Puerto Rico’s new natural resources secretary has shelved an investigation into allegations of illegal construction in a protected area on the island’s southwest coast.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden levies new sanctions against Russian energy sector, but it's up to Trump whether to keep them
item.description: President Joe Biden's administration says it's expanding sanctions against Russia's critically important energy sector over its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canada's Liberal Party to decide on March 9 who will succeed Trudeau as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's governing Liberal Party will announce the country's next prime minister on March 9 after a leadership vote that follows the resignation of Justin Trudeau this week, party leaders said late Thursday. Trudeau remains prime minister until a new leader is chosen.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia open to Trump, Putin meeting: Kremlin
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is ready to meet President-elect Trump without conditions, the Kremlin said Friday, but is waiting for more specifics following the U.S. presidential inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: South Korea’s acting leader accepts resignation of presidential security chief
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s acting leader on Friday accepted the resignation of the chief of the presidential security service, Park Jong-joon, as he faced police questioning over how his forces blocked law enforcement efforts to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol last week.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's victory could give boost to Brazil's Bolsonaro's re-election hopes amid court ban on him running
item.description: Brazil's supreme court has prevented Jair Bolsonaro from running again in the 2026 presidential election, yet the former president and his team are hoping the incoming Trump administration might help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Venezuela's Maduro takes new oath amid protests and international rebuke
item.description: Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro has been sworn in to serve a third term despite credible evidence that his opponent won the July 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Regina Garcia Cano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: An albatross couple shares egg duty in this captivating low-drama reality show
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — It’s a reality show about a loving couple waiting to welcome their new arrival, watched by thousands of ardent fans.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK foreign policy chief says Trump is right to urge higher military spending from NATO
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Europe’s security “is on a knife-edge” and President-elect Donald Trump is right to say NATO member nations must increase military spending, Britain’s top diplomat said Thursday. Foreign Secretary David Lammy said that “the post-Cold War peace is well and truly over.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris, Emhoff cancel trip to Singapore, Middle East, Germany amid California fires
item.description: Vice President Harris and second gentleman Doug Emhoff announced their decision to cancel their upcoming international trips on Thursday in the wake of the Los Angeles wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Italy’s foreign minister heads to Syria to encourage post-Assad transition
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said he would travel to Syria on Friday to encourage the country's transition following the ouster of President Bashar Assad by Islamist insurgents, and appealed on Europe to review its sanctions on Damascus now that the political situation has cha
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scientists drill nearly 2 miles down to pull 1.2 million-year-old ice core from Antarctic
item.description: An international team of scientists announced Thursday they’ve successfully drilled one of the oldest ice cores yet, penetrating nearly 2 miles (2.8 kilometers) to Antarctic bedrock to reach ice they say is at least 1.2 million years old.
item.dc:creator: PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel sends report to UN on 'brutal' treatment used by terrorists against hostages in Gaza
item.description: In a new report sent to the U.N. by Israel's health ministry, freed hostages reveal systematic abuse by Hamas, as 99 remain in captivity enduring brutal physical and psychological torment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baby girl born on packed migrant boat heading for Spain's Canary Islands
item.description: A baby girl was born on a packed migrant boat headed for Spain's Lanzarote island in the Canary Islands. Authorities said the baby and her mother were in good condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Attack on Chad's presidential palace leaves 19 dead
item.description: N'DJAMENA, Chad (AP) — An attack on Chad's presidential palace left 18 assailants dead and six in custody, with one soldier killed and three wounded, state media reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cold spell claims first two lives in El Paso-Juarez area
item.description: Tuesday’s death of a homeless man presumably due to hypothermia has set off alarms in a border city with a large floating population of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico offers protection to famed singer after drug cartel death threats
item.description: Mexican authorities are offering protection to famed regional Mexican singer Natanael Cano and other artists after a drug cartel publicly threatened them, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers introduce ‘Georgian Nightmare Non-Recognition Act' in warning to Putin allied government
item.description: Senior Republican and Democratic representatives will introduce a bill on Wednesday prohibiting recognition of the Georgian Dream government amid daily protests against the new government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 9 miners are trapped in a coal mine in India's northeastern Assam state
item.description: Indian authorities have summoned the army to help rescue at least nine workers who are trapped inside a flooded coal mine in the country's northeastern Assam state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jean-Marie Le Pen, French far-right leader known for fiery rhetoric against immigration, dies at 96
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Jean-Marie Le Pen, the founder of France’s far-right National Front who was known for fiery rhetoric against immigration and multiculturalism that earned him both staunch supporters and widespread condemnation, died Tuesday. He was 96.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, SYLVIE CORBET and ELAINE GANLEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to announce resignation: Reports
item.description: It remains unclear whether Justin Trudeau will leave immediately or stay on as prime minister until a new leader is selected, the report added.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: China fires shot across Trump's bow with defense sanctions
item.description: It could be a sign of a brewing tit-for-tat as the two superpowers jockey for economic and military leverage.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation
